_meta.description: WC2026 qualifying campaign records for all 48 teams
_meta.fields: played, won, drawn, lost, goalsFor, goalsAgainst, goalDiff, method

Argentina.note: Reigning world champions. Messi carried qualifying with 12 goals. Dominant in CONMEBOL.

France.note: Won UEFA qualifying group. Mbappe scored 9. Lost once to Austria, drew with Israel.

Spain.note: Dominant group winner. Lamine Yamal (when fit) outstanding. Best defensive record in UEFA qualifying.

England.note: Qualified top of group despite Foden/Palmer controversies. Kane 8 qualifying goals. Inconsistent at times.

Brazil.note: Ancelotti's first campaign. Struggled early, finished 3rd in CONMEBOL. Vinícius/Raphinha carried attack. Rodrygo absence hurt.

Portugal.note: Most goals in UEFA qualifying. Ronaldo 11 goals. Dominant from start to finish. Only loss was dead rubber.

Netherlands.note: Scrappy qualification via playoffs. De Jong injury concerns throughout. Gakpo and Depay stepped up.

Colombia.note: 2nd in CONMEBOL. Díaz outstanding — 10 goals from wide. Copa America 2024 finalists form carried into qualifying.

Croatia.note: Stuttered through playoffs. Modrić still pulling strings at 40. Gvardiol injury affected form. Lowest-ranked qualifier from UEFA.

Belgium.note: Won group despite transitioning from golden generation. De Bruyne still key. Lost to France and Netherlands.

Germany.note: Dominant. Havertz 9 goals. Wirtz hit form post-Liverpool transfer. Euro 2024 hosts momentum carried into qualifying.

Morocco.note: Won CAF Round 4 group. Best defensive record in Africa. AFCON 2023 runners-up. En-Nesyri top scorer with 8.

Uruguay.note: 4th in CONMEBOL. Darwin Núñez 11 goals. Valverde dominant in midfield. Inconsistent away from home.

Mexico.note: Host nation (automatic qualification). Played CONCACAF Nations League as preparation. Gimenez 10 goals. Nations League Final vs USA won 2-1.

USA.note: Host nation. Won CONCACAF Nations League group stage. Pochettino building cohesion. Pulisic 8 Nations League goals.

Japan.note: Dominant in AFC 3rd round. Scored 26, conceded only 5. Minamino/Mitoma absences hurt late qualifiers. Still won comfortably.

Switzerland.note: Comfortable group winners. Xhaka orchestrated play. Embolo 7 goals. Reliable and consistent throughout.

Senegal.note: Won CAF group. Koulibaly injury concern (thigh) late in campaign. Mane retired but squad depth shown. Diatta 7 goals.

South Korea.note: Won AFC group. Son 8 goals. Lee Kang-in's PSG form reflected in qualifying performances. Kim Min-jae marshalled defence superbly.

Australia.note: Won AFC playoff. Mathew Ryan key in shootouts. Mitchell Duke physical threat. Relied on defensive organization throughout.

Sweden.note: Won UEFA playoff. Gyökeres 15 qualifying goals — extraordinary. Isak added 8 more. Two-striker partnership is devastating.

Norway.note: Won group. Haaland 9 goals despite missing 3 matches injured. Ødegaard 7 assists. Team system sometimes misfired without Haaland.

Austria.note: Rangnick's best qualifying campaign ever. Baumgartner 12 goals. Alaba absent — won without their captain. Excellent team pressing.

Turkiye.note: Won group. Arda Güler 9 goals including five stunning long-range strikes. Çalhanoğlu marshalled midfield. Euro 2024 QF form continued.

Iran.note: Won AFC group convincingly. Taremi 8 goals. Azmoun returned from injury to add 5 more. Best Asian qualifying record after Japan.

Ecuador.note: 5th in CONMEBOL. Caicedo and Hincapié standout performers. Valencia (36) still scoring. Patchy form but got through.

Scotland.note: Won group including a famous win vs Spain. McTominay 10 qualifying goals from midfield. Robertson led brilliantly as captain.

Czechia.note: Won UEFA playoff. Schick fit enough for final run. Souček led from the back with 5 goals. Beat Wales and Ukraine in playoffs.

Tunisia.note: Won CAF playoff. Disciplined and hard to score against. Msakni key in tight qualifiers. Defensive quality their trademark.

Egypt.note: Won CAF group. Salah 9 goals — without him they would have struggled. El-Shenawy kept 5 clean sheets. Qualifying carried by Salah.

Qatar.note: Won AFC Asian Cup 2023 and 2024. Direct qualifier as AFC representative. Afif 12 tournament goals in Asian Cups. Vastly improved since 2022 disaster.

Ivory Coast.note: Won CAF group. AFCON 2023 champions. Haller 8 goals. Kessié and Youssouf Fofana dominated midfield in African qualifiers.

Canada.note: Host nation. Nations League competitive. Jonathan David 11 goals in preparation. Davies hamstring a cloud over entire camp.

Bosnia and Herzegovina.note: Won UEFA playoff. Demirović 10 goals. Transition post-Džeko era. Inconsistent but scraped through. Won 3-2 on aggregate in playoff.

Paraguay.note: Won intercontinental playoff (beat Asian team). 6th in CONMEBOL. Almirón key. Goalless GD shows inconsistency — draws specialist.

Haiti.note: Won CONCACAF playoff. First WC since 1974. Pierrot and Nazon crucial. Shock qualifiers — defending very organized at CONCACAF level.

Scotland.note: Won group including a famous win vs Spain. McTominay 10 qualifying goals from midfield. Robertson led brilliantly as captain.

Curacao.note: Won CONCACAF playoff. Historic first WC. Bacuna key. Qualified despite negative GD — peaked in crucial playoff matches.

Sweden.note: Gyökeres 15 qualifying goals. Isak added 8 more. Won playoff convincingly. Two-striker system is devastating.

New Zealand.note: Won OFC qualification. Chris Wood 7 goals. Easy opposition in Oceania — standard drops dramatically vs World Cup level teams.

Cape Verde.note: Won CAF playoff. Historic first WC. Rodrigues key. Organized defensively; scored in clutch moments when it mattered.

Saudi Arabia.note: Won AFC playoff. Al-Dawsari 7 goals. Mancini's tactical structure improved their discipline. Al-Owais key in tight matches.

Iraq.note: Won AFC playoff. First WC since 1986. Hussein key striker. Domestically based squad performed beyond expectations in Asian qualifiers.

Algeria.note: Won CAF group. Mahrez 7 goals despite Saudi league step-down. Belmadi's system well-drilled. AFCON 2023 runners-up form translated.

Jordan.note: Won AFC playoff. Asian Cup Final 2024 appearance built confidence. Al-Taamari 6 goals. First WC after impressive Asian form.

DR Congo.note: Won CAF group. Bakambu and Mbemba (both Marseille) gave them European-level quality. Physical and organized in African qualifiers.

Uzbekistan.note: Won AFC playoff. Shomurodov (Roma) 8 goals. Masharipov creative force. Historic first WC — dominated Central Asian opponents.

Colombia.note: 2nd in CONMEBOL. Copa America 2024 finalists. Díaz 10 goals from wide. James still producing assists at 34. Very consistent campaign.

Ghana.note: Won CAF group. Kudus 8 goals. Semenyo and Jordan Ayew supported well. Partey fit enough for qualifying — midfield quality clear.

Panama.note: Won CONCACAF playoff. Carrasquilla creative force. Physical and defensive — 4 clean sheets in playoffs. Beat Mexico in CONCACAF to qualify.

South Africa.note: Won CAF playoff. Ronwen Williams outstanding — made critical saves. Tau and Zwane key attackers. AFCON third-place finish showed quality.

Australia.note: Won AFC playoff. Ryan heroic in shootouts (again). Duke physical threat. Popovic's organized setup has improved defensive record.
